Article Title: In blow to Congress, Jharkhand cross-voting hands NDA-backed Parimal Nathwani Rajya Sabha seat
Article Text:
In blow to Congress, Jharkhand cross-voting hands NDA-backed Parimal Nathwani Rajya Sabha seat

RANCHI/AIZAWL: NDA-backed Independent Parimal Nathwani crushed Congr-ess's hopes of a Rajya Sabha berth from Jharkhand, spr-inging a surprise in Thursd-ay's polls that exposed cracks within INDIA bloc ranks, while governing Zoram People's Movement (ZPM) comfortably pocketed Mizoram's lone Upper House seat.Contests in MP, Karnataka and Rajasthan ended June 11, the day withdrawal of nominations closed. BJP won three seats in MP, Congress three and BJP one in Karnataka, while BJP secured two seats and Congress one in Rajasthan - all unopposed.Jharkhand's contest went down to wire. In the 81-member assembly, INDIA bloc held a clear edge with 56 MLAs - 34 from JMM, 16 from Congress, four from RJD and two from CPI-ML Liberation - against NDA's 24. Under a 1:1 seat-sharing pact, JMM fielded former minister Baidyanath Ram and Congress nominated Pranav Jha.Ram won 30 votes and Nathwani secured 28, enough to clinch the second berth. Jha finished third with 20 votes. Three ballots were declared invalid."An Independent candidate has once again resorted to horse-trading under BJP's patronage. While our own MLAs and four from JMM remained intact, MLAs from RJD and CPI-ML did not support us," Congress's Jharkhand affairs in-charge K Raju alleged.CPI-ML rejected Congress's allegations. State secretary Manoj Bhakt said both its MLAs voted for Jha. "Congress is resorting to baseless rhetoric to conceal its own weaknesses," he said.BJP hailed the result as legislators voting according to their conscience.In Mizoram, ZPM candidate K Laltluangkima (63) cruised to victory. Returning officer Zothansanga Ralte said the turnout was 36 in the 40-member assembly. ZPM's W Chhuanawma did not vote because of health issues, while two BJP MLAs and the lone Congress legislator abstained. Mizo National Front, which has 10 MLAs, fielded Zothansangi Hmar. Sources said Laltluangkima secured 26 votes against Hmar's 10.
Article Summary: NDA-backed Independent Parimal Nathwani crushed Congr-ess's hopes of a Rajya Sabha berth from Jharkhand. Zoram People's Movement (ZPM) comfortably pocketed Mizoram's lone Upper House seat. Contests in MP, Karnataka and Rajasthan ended June 11, the day withdrawal of nominations closed.

Article Title: Calcutta high court refuses to stay Ritabrata Banerjee's appointment as Bengal LoP
Article Text:
Calcutta HC declined to stay the appointment of rebel Trinamool MLA Ritabrata Banerjee as LoP

KOLKATA: Calcutta high court declined to stay Thursday the appointment of rebel Trinamool MLA Ritabrata Banerjee as leader of the opposition in the Bengal assembly, handing his camp an early courtroom edge on opening day of the first session under BJP govt.Hearing a petition by Trinamool Congress MLA Sobhandeb Chattopadhyay, Justice Krishna Rao refused interim relief and pointed out that a resolution submitted to the speaker by TMC national general secretary Abhishek Banerjee nominating Sobhandeb as LoP remained "in dispute". The court also flagged an Alipore court order of June 15 that stayed Ritabrata's expulsion from TMC until June 30. The high court fixed July 28 for the next hearing.Ritabrata, Sandipan Saha (who is now deputy LoP), and several other rebel MLAs backing BJP-led NDA have alleged their signatures were forged on a May 6 letter sent to the assembly speaker proposing Sobhandeb's name as LoP - a case making headlines as "signgate" and being investigated by Bengal police CID.Sandipan called HC's refusal to intervene a "moral victory". "Ritabrata and I had initiated this fight. We brought the alleged signature irregularities in TMC's letter to the speaker's notice.The issue then went to court. Today's victory in HC confirms that law is on our side," he said.HC's refusal to hit pause has left Ritabrata firmly in the saddle for now, but courtroom battles and political trench warfare inside TMC show few signs of easing. "Of 80 Trinamool MLAs, 65 are with us. Of the remaining 15, one is in jail. For the time being, 14 are on the other side. Let us wait and watch," Ritabrata said.The ruling failed to cool tempers inside the assembly. Five Mamata Banerjee loyalists - Sobhandeb, Kunal Ghosh, Madan Mitra, Abdul Rahim Boxi and Asoke Deb - met CM Suvendu Adhikari and demanded separate seating arrangements, making clear they would not share benches with rebel MLAs."We will not sit with them. We also told CM Adhikari that we will not seek time allotments to speak from Ritabrata. He agreed and asked us to coordinate with parliamentary affairs minister Shankar Ghosh," Kunal said.He said Sobhandeb appeared to receive symbolic backing during the governor's address. "CM was not only courteous to Sobhandeb, but we did notice that during the governor's address, the seat to the left of the speaker, traditionally reserved for LoP, was kept for Sobhandeb today," he said.
Article Summary: Calcutta high court declines to stay appointment of rebel Trinamool MLA Ritabrata Banerjee as leader of the opposition in the Bengal assembly. RitAbrata, Sandipan Saha (who is now deputy LoP), and several other rebel MLAs backing BJP-led NDA have alleged their signatures were forged on a May 6 letter.

Article Title: Historic first for Mizoram's ZPM as K Laltluangkima enters Rajya Sabha
Article Text:
Laltluangkima got 26 votes in the 40-member assembly. (IANS file photo)

NEW DELHI: Mizoram's ruling Zoram People's Movement (ZPM) on Thursday created history by securing its first-ever representation in the Rajya Sabha. Senior leader K Laltluangkima won the election to the state's lone Upper House seat.Laltluangkima polled 26 votes in the 40-member assembly, defeating opposition Mizo National Front (MNF) candidate Zothansangi Hmar, who secured all 10 votes cast by her party's legislators, news agency PTI reported.Three MLAs, BJP legislators K Beichhua and K Hrahmo, and Congress MLA C Ngunlianchunga, abstained from voting, while ZPM legislator W Chhuanawma could not cast his vote due to health reasons.The ZPM, which came to power in the 2023 assembly elections, currently has 27 MLAs in the House. The MNF has 10 members, while the BJP and Congress have two and one legislators, respectively.The victory marks a major milestone for the ZPM, founded in 2017, as Laltluangkima becomes the party's first Rajya Sabha MP. He is also the eighth person from Mizoram to be elected to the Upper House since 1972, when the state was a Union Territory.The outgoing Rajya Sabha member is the MNF's K Vanlalvena.Born in October 1963, Laltluangkima holds a Bachelor of Commerce degree from St Anthony's College and later studied Christian Communication in England.He also earned a Master's degree in Plumbing from an institution in Maryland, US.Laltluangkima entered politics in 1997 through the MNF (Nationalist), which later became the Zoram Nationalist Party (ZNP) under Lalduhoma. He is currently the member secretary of the ZPM's political affairs committee and the party's official spokesperson.
Article Summary: Senior leader K Laltluangkima won the election to the state's lone Upper House seat. He polled 26 votes in the 40-member assembly, defeating opposition Mizo National Front (MNF) candidate Zothansangi Hmar. The victory marks a major milestone for the ZPM, founded in 2017.

Article Title: Karnataka MLC polls: Congress wins 5 seats amid cross-voting; BJP gets 2 seats
Article Text:
This was first electoral contest since DK Shivakumar assumed CM office.

NEW DELHI: The ruling Congress won five of the seven seats in the Karnataka legislative council elections on Thursday in the first electoral contest since the new government led by chief minister DK Shivakumar assumed office.The BJP secured the remaining two, news agency PTI reported.Congress candidates Thippannappa Kamknoor, PV Mohan, BK Hariprasad, Shivanna BS and Vinay Karthik Prakash were declared elected. The BJP's Lingaraj Patil and Raghu R also emerged victorious, while the JD(S)'s lone candidate, Govindaraju, lost the contest.According to PTI sources, there were instances of cross-voting by legislators from both the BJP and the JD(S), with Congress candidates reportedly securing more votes than anticipated.Voting for the seven legislative council seats was held at the Vidhana Soudha in Bengaluru, where eight candidates were in the fray. The seats fell vacant as the terms of seven sitting MLCs are set to end on June 30.The retiring members include Congress leaders Naseer Ahmed, Tippannappa and BK Hariprasad; BJP leaders N Nagaraju (MTB), Prathap Simha Nayak K and Sunil Vallyapur; and JD(S) leader Govindaraju.
Article Summary: The ruling Congress won five of the seven seats in the Karnataka legislative council elections on Thursday. The BJP secured the remaining two, news agency PTI reported. This was first electoral contest since the new government led by chief minister DK Shivakumar assumed office.

Article Title: Watch: Eggs thrown at ex-Bengal minister Udayan Guha, latest TMC leader to face attack
Article Text:
Ex-Bengal minister Udayan Guha (wearing helmet) outside court in Dinhata, West Bengal

NEW DELHI: Eggs were pelted on Thursday at Udayan Guha, a Trinamool Congress (TMC) leader and former West Bengal minister, marking the latest incident of a TMC leader being targeted with eggs after the party was voted out of power in the assembly elections held in April.The incident occurred a day after his arrest in an alleged extortion case.Visuals showed Guha, clad in a white kurta and wearing a helmet, being escorted out of a court in Dinhata by police personnel holding shields.An egg was suddenly hurled at him, but it struck his helmet.Trinamool MP and the party’s second-in-command, Abhishek Banerjee, was the first leader to be targeted days after the TMC’s electoral defeat, which brought an end to his aunt and party supremo Mamata Banerjee’s 15-year tenure as West Bengal chief minister.Senior TMC MP Kalyan Banerjee was struck with eggs a day later.The Trinamool has accused the BJP , which now governs West Bengal, of orchestrating the attacks on its leaders. The BJP, however, has maintained that the incidents reflect public anger over what it describes as years of TMC "misrule."Guha, son of veteran Forward Bloc leader Kamal Guha, joined the Trinamool Congress in 2016 and later served as a minister in the Mamata Banerjee government.
Article Summary: Eggs were pelted on Thursday at Udayan Guha, a Trinamool Congress (TMC) leader and former West Bengal minister. Incident occurred a day after his arrest in an alleged extortion case. It is the latest incident of a TMC leader being targeted with eggs after the party was voted out of power.

Article Title: Shiv Sena (UBT) crisis: Pending verdict in 2006 Pawanraje Nimbalkar murder case adds another twist to MP son’s dilemma
Article Text:
A two-decade-old murder case involving the father of a Shiv Sena (UBT) MP has become a flashpoint in the party's internal conflict. (Omprakash Nimbalkar (L) , Padamsinh Patil (R))





MUMBAI: Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Omraje Nimbalkar has so far neither confirmed nor denied reports that he is part of the rebel group moving towards the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena.He has maintained that he was not in Delhi on Wednesday or Thursday and skipped the party's parliamentary meeting convened by the Uddhav Thackeray camp."I have not gone to Delhi. I have decided to make my stand clear after June 20," Nimbalkar said.His remarks come amid growing speculation that six Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs are preparing to break away from the party. Sena (UBT) MP Sanjay Raut has alleged that pressure is being exerted on Nimbalkar because of the impending verdict in the murder case of his father, former Congress leader Pawanraje Nimbalkar."Twenty years ago, his father was murdered and the trial is at a crucial stage. There is pressure on Omraje Nimbalkar that if he joins the Shinde Sena, he will get relief from the court," Raut alleged, without providing evidence.The case pertains to the June 3, 2006 assassination of Pawanraje Nimbalkar and his driver, Samad Kazi, near Kalamboli in Navi Mumbai. The killings triggered a major political controversy in Maharashtra and led to a prolonged legal battle.A special CBI court in Mumbai is expected to deliver its verdict on Saturday in the high-profile double murder case.The judgment was originally scheduled for June 17 but was deferred as the court required additional time to complete the order.Among the accused is former Maharashtra minister and senior NCP leader Padamsinh Patil, brother of deputy CM Sunetra Pawar. The prosecution has alleged that the murders were the result of political and business rivalries, including disputes linked to the management of Terna Sugar Factory and the 2004 assembly election contest between Patil and Nimbalkar.The case was initially investigated by Kalamboli police and later by the Navi Mumbai crime branch. However, following a petition by Nimbalkar's widow Anandibai, the Bombay high court transferred the probe to the CBI in 2008, describing the state police investigation as "desultory and lackadaisical". The trial was later shifted from Alibaug to Mumbai on the Supreme Court's directions.The CBI alleges that Pawanraje Nimbalkar's murder was carried out through a contract killing. Its case relies on the testimony of approver Parasmal Jain, forensic and ballistic evidence, call detail records, and the statements of more than 120 witnesses examined during the trial.Omraje Nimbalkar, 42, is the sitting Lok Sabha MP from Dharashiv and one of the prominent faces of Shiv Sena (UBT) in Marathwada. Before entering Parliament, he served as an MLA from Osmanabad. He entered politics in his early twenties and built a reputation for grassroots mobilisation and an aggressive political style.As speculation over the Sena split intensifies, Nimbalkar's next move is being closely watched, particularly with the verdict in his father's murder case due within days.
Article Summary: Omraje Nimbalkar has so far neither confirmed nor denied reports that he is part of the rebel group moving towards the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena. He has maintained that he was not in Delhi on Wednesday or Thursday and skipped the party's parliamentary meeting convened by the Uddhav Thackeray camp. A special CBI court in Mumbai is expected to deliver its verdict on Saturday in the high- profile double murder case.

Article Title: Mumbai Pocso case accused gets temporary bail to write NEET retest
Article Text:
The student has sought the relief to prepare and sit for the NEET exam scheduled for June 21. (Representational Photo)





MUMBAI: A special Pocso court on Wednesday granted temporary bail to an 18-year-old student currently held in judicial custody at Taloja Central Prison, allowing him to appear for the upcoming NEET re-examination.The student, who faces charges under the BNS and the Pocso Act , sought the relief to prepare and sit for the exam scheduled for June 21.The victim stated she had no objection to bail being granted, provided the accused gave an assurance that he would not defame or threaten her family.“ ….it is found that there are perspicuous valid grounds for release of accused on provisional temporary bail so as to enable him to appear for exam,” the special judge said.
Article Summary: The student, who faces charges under the BNS and the Pocso Act, sought the relief to prepare and sit for the exam scheduled for June 21. The victim stated she had no objection to bail being granted, provided the accused gave an assurance that he would not defame or threaten her family.

Article Title: MLC polls: Mahayuti eyes big gains in Marathwada
Article Text:
Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

:

TOI





The elections to four Local Authorities’ Constituencies in Marathwada on Thursday witnessed an average of over 99% polling with Mahayuti leaders expressing hopes of massive wins due to claimed cross-voting from the MVA camp.Held under the shadow of ‘Operation Tiger’ aimed at poaching of Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs, the MLC polls allegedly saw confusion among a section of MVA voters associated with three MPs from Marathwada who are expected to ditch Uddhav Thackeray Among four constituencies that went for poll, Aurangabad-Jalna Local Authorities’ Constituency reported 98.9% polling whereas both Nanded Local Authorities’ Constituency and Parbhani-Hingoli Local Authorities’ Constituency registered 99.8% voting while the figure for Osmanabad-Latur-Beed Local Authorities’ Constituency was recorded at 99.5%. BJP Minister for OBC welfare Atul Save said the NDA will have a clean sweep in Marathwada and at the state-level in the MLC polls. “Mahayuti’s victory in Marathwada will be magnificent due to large-scale cross-voting from the MVA camp. Sena (UBT) MPs are joining the NDA to fulfill aspirations of development of their respective constituencies and such a switch will offer the Mahayuti early gains in MLC polls,” he toldCongress general secretary Jitendra Dehade said the voters will show opportunistic leaders their place in the upcoming elections.“The claims of cross-voting are baseless and we will give a tough fight,” he said.Senior Sena (UBT) leaders, however, refused to comment. Among major developments in the region, in Parbhani-Hingoli constituency, Congress had issued a whip asking its elected representatives to vote for Krushna Patil, the MVA candidate and son of Nagesh Patil Ashtikar, who is believed to be one of the poached MPs under the “Operation Tiger “.The official results for the MLC polls will be declared on June 22
Article Summary: The elections to four Local Authorities’ Constituencies in Marathwada on Thursday witnessed an average of over 99% polling. Mahayuti leaders expressed hopes of massive wins due to claimed cross-voting from the MVA camp. The official results for the MLC polls will be declared on June 22.

Article Title: Special CBI court’s verdict in Pawanraje Nimbalkar murder case on June 20
Article Text:
It is the CBI’s case that the murders were executed through a contract killing of Rs 25 lakh due to political rivalry.

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar:





A special CBI court in Mumbai will deliver its verdict on June 20 in the two-decade-old murder case of Dharashiv Congress leader Pawanraje Nimbalkar and his driver, Samad Kazi. Among the eight accused facing trial is former Maharashtra home minister and ex-NCP MP Padamsinh Patil, who is also Nimbalkar’s cousin.The case was listed for pronouncement of judgment on Tuesday, but the special judge said he would need another two to three days to complete the order.Nimbalkar and Kazi were shot dead on June 3, 2006, while travelling from Mumbai to Osmanabad (now Dharashiv). Two assailants intercepted their car at Kalamboli in Navi Mumbai and opened fire, killing both on the spot. The probe was initially conducted by the Navi Mumbai police before being transferred to the CBI following directions from the Bombay high court.Patil was arrested in June 2009 and granted bail in Sept the same year. The trial commenced in July 2011, during which the special court examined 128 witnesses.Nimbalkar’s son, Omprakash, is currently a Lok Sabha member from Shiv Sena (UBT). He was present in court on Tuesday, and said the families of the two victims had been waiting for justice for over 20 years.“We now only expect justice. An ordinary citizen has to struggle immensely to get justice,” he said.Omprakash said he had approached the HC seeking a CBI probe into the case, maintaining that the murders were politically motivated. He alleged that political rivalry had intensified after his father lost the 2004 assembly election by a narrow margin of 484 votes. “This was a pre-planned murder carried out to eliminate a political opponent,” he alleged.It is the CBI’s case that the murders were executed through a contract killing of Rs 25 lakh due to political rivalry.
Article Summary: The case was listed for pronouncement of judgment on Tuesday, but the special judge said he would need another two to three days to complete the order. Nimbalkar and Kazi were shot dead on June 3, 2006, while travelling from Mumbai to Osmanabad (now Dharashiv) Two assailants intercepted their car at Kalamboli in Navi Mumbai and opened fire.

Article Title: Sena (UBT) neta seeks independent probe into postmortem of headmaster Borse
Article Text:
Danve demanded the appointment of an independent committee to probe events that allegedly took place during the postmortem conducted after Borse’s death

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

:





Shiv Sena (UBT) functionary and MLC Ambadas Danve has written to Maharashtra chief minister Devendra Fadnavis seeking a high-level and impartial inquiry into alleged irregularities and suspicious circumstances surrounding the postmortem of Ellora-based headmaster Suresh Borse.In his letter sent on Sunday, Danve demanded the appointment of an independent committee to probe events that allegedly took place during the postmortem conducted after Borse's death. He said several questions had arisen regarding a meeting between the Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar district superintendent of police and the dean of the Government Medical College and Hospital (GMCH) while the autopsy process was underway.Danve stated the purpose of the meeting remained unclear and had given rise to public suspicion. He sought clarification on whether the doctors conducting the postmortem were called for the meeting and whether any attempt was made to exert pressure, influence or intimidation on the medical officers involved in preparing the autopsy report.The Sena (UBT) functionary also questioned whether the meeting could have had any bearing on the impartiality of the postmortem findings. Danve said it was essential that the truth behind the matter be established through an independent and unbiased inquiry so that public confidence in the administrative and investigative machinery remained intact.He urged state govt to immediately constitute a special investigation committee comprising senior administrative officials, judicial officers and independent medical experts to examine all aspects of the case.
Article Summary: Shiv Sena (UBT) functionary and MLC Ambadas Danve has written to Maharashtra chief minister Devendra Fadnavis seeking a high-level and impartial inquiry. Danve demanded the appointment of an independent committee to probe events that allegedly took place during the postmortem conducted after Borse’s death.

Article Title: Amit Shah to address political rallies in Kolhapur on Saturday
Article Text:
Union minister Amit Shah

Kolhapur

:





Union home minister Amit Shah will lay the foundation work of new Pradakshina Marg and conservation work of Parivar Devata Mandir at Kolhapur’s Mahalaxmi temple on Saturday morning.Chief minister Devendra Fadnavis , deputy CMs Eknath Shinde and Sunetra Pawar will also attend the function. It is Shah’s first visit to Maharashtra after Shiv Sena (UBT) staring at another split and after the delegation of CM and both DCM’s met him at Delhi to raise concerns over sugar cooperative industry.District collector Vijay Rathod, while making the announcement about Shah’s visit on Thursday, said, “The development projects will be carried out under Shri Mahalaxmi Temple Development Plan. The new Pradakshina Marg will ensure safety and convenient darshan for the devotees. The conservation and beautification work of Parivar Devata Mandir from the premise of the temple will also be carried out.”The district authorities said that Shah will arrive on Friday evening in Kolhapur. After Saturday morning’s event at Mahalaxmi temple the home minister will address political rallies at the Police Parade Ground in Kolhapur and then at Ichalkaranji. The rallies are arranged by BJP to thank the voters who helped BJP and its allies in the 2024 all assembly elections.Ahead of Shah’s visit, the roads have been cleaned and smoothened. The traffic diversions will be in place for movement of Shah’s convoy. Shah will leave for Delhi around 4pm.
Article Summary: Amit Shah will lay the foundation work of new Pradakshina Marg and conservation work of Parivar Devata Mandir at Kolhapur’s Mahalaxmi temple on Saturday morning. Chief minister Devendra Fadnavis, deputy CMs Eknath Shinde and Sunetra Pawar will also attend the function.

Article Title: Teen who rammed SUV into woman before crashing into 6 vehicles in Navi Mumbai booked for drunk driving
Article Text:
Drunk teen SUV driver drags woman 50m, hits 6 parked vehicles

Watch CCTV Captures Delhi Teen’s Speeding SUV Seconds Before Fatal Dwarka Crash CCTV Captures Delhi Teen’s Speeding SUV Seconds Before Fatal Dwarka Crash

NAVI MUMBAI: The CBD-Belapur police have invoked section 185 of the Motor Vehicle Act for drunk driving against the accused 19-year-old SUV driver.The teenager had hit and dragged a woman pedestrian for 50 metres and caused her severe injuries and then rammed into six vehicles parked on the roadside in Artist village, sector-8 on Friday.Arun Pawar, senior inspector of CBD-Belapur said, the accused SUV driver Vikram Kamble's medical test report received from NMMC hospital, Nerul confirmed that he was under the influence of alcohol while driving.Subsequently, relevant section 185 Of Motor Vehicle Act that penalizes drunk driving were invoked against him and will be mentioned in the chargesheet that will be submitted in the court.The woman Mohini Shinde (35), who worked as househelp, sustained severe head injuries and bruises on her face and near the knees is recuperating at MGM hospital, CBD-Belapur and is out of danger, informed doctors.The accident took place in the residential locality of Artist village in Sector-8, CBD-Belapur around 5.30 pm on Friday. Police said the speeding SUV hit the woman from behind while she was walking close to the pavement and dragged her for nearly 50 metres before crashing into six vehicles parked along the roadside.Local residents rushed the injured woman to MGM Hospital, where she received prompt treatment. The incident was also captured on CCTV cameras installed in the area.Police said the SUV driver Vikram Gangadhar Kamble (19) was accompanied by his relative Akash Chandrakant Kamble (26), a private chauffeur. Both were detained by locals at the spot before the police arrived, following which an FIR was registered for rash and negligent driving and damage to property.
Article Summary: The CBD-Belapur police have invoked section 185 of the Motor Vehicle Act for drunk driving against the accused 19-year-old SUV driver. The teenager had hit and dragged a woman pedestrian for 50 metres and caused her severe injuries and then rammed into six vehicles parked on the roadside. The woman Mohini Shinde (35), who worked as househelp, sustained severe head injuries and bruises on her face and near the knees.

Article Title: Sena (UBT) makes desperate attempt to keep back two MPs
Article Text: Sena (UBT)‘s MLC Ambadas Danve and MLA Kailash Patil have been told to reach out to Osmanabad MP Omprakash Rajenimbalkar and Mumbai North-East MP Sanjay Dina Patil
Article Summary: Sena (UBT)’s MLC Ambadas Danve and MLA Kailash Patil have been told to reach out to Osmanabad MP Omprakash Rajenimbalkar and Mumbai North-East MP Sanjay Dina Patil.

Article Title: 3 held, Pune Rural cops crack 20 cases
Article Text:
The Pune Rural police managed to crack 20 cases of burglaries, robberies and dacoities with the arrests, while recovering gold and silver jewellery and a motorcycle together worth Rs25 lakh from the accused

Pune:





Three youths have been arrested and their minor accomplice detained for robbing cash and jewellery collectively worth Rs7.1 lakh from five houses in Daund taluka on June 10.The Pune Rural police managed to crack 20 cases of burglaries, robberies and dacoities with the arrests, while recovering gold and silver jewellery and a motorcycle together worth Rs25 lakh from the accused. The aforementioned crimes took place in Babirnagar, Khorwadi and Alegaon villages in Daund taluka, about 80 km from Pune.The youths were caught and the accompllice detained after raids in Ahilyanagar and Beed districts.The arrested youths were identified as Ganesh Kale (22) and Kishore Bhosale (19) of Ahilyanagar and Vishal Kale (21) of Beed district. In most instances, the accused and their accomplices had assaulted victims with sharp weapons and thrashed them with sticks before robbing them.Pune Rural superintendent of police Sandeep Singh Gill had formed six teams to probe the cases. The teams collected detailed descriptions of the accused, alerted informants and checked CCTV camera footage along 50km stretch of the scenes of crime.“The footage confirmed that the dacoits had entered the district from and returned to Ahilyanagar. The police ascertained the identities of four accused, who were reportedly criminals with cases registered against them,” Gill said.“Additionally, the youths never returned to their houses after the crimes. They either hid in farms or changed their appearance to work in brick kilns. During sustained questioning, the accused admitted to having committed robberies and burglaries at 12 spots in Pune Rural and eight in Ahilyanagar district. Police are looking for their accomplices,” Gill added.
Article Summary: The Pune Rural police managed to crack 20 cases of burglaries, robberies and dacoities with the arrests. The aforementioned crimes took place in Babirnagar, Khorwadi and Alegaon villages in Daund taluka, about 80 km from Pune. The youths were caught and the accomplice detained after raids in Ahilyanagar and Beed districts.

Article Title: Police arrest chain snatcher in Ravet, six cases solved
Article Text:
Picture for representation purpose only

Pune:





The Ravet police have arrested a chain snatcher with a criminal record and solved five chain-snatching cases and a vehicle theft case. They have recovered gold jewellery worth Rs1.65 lakh and the stolen motorcycle, collectively worth Rs3 lakh, from the accused, Aditya Manlor (19), a resident of Shivajinagar. Manlor has six cases of chain and mobile handset snatching registered against him.A Ravet police team led by senior police inspector Nitin Fatangare took the accused into custody after examining CCTV footage. “During questioning, Manlor admitted to have snatched gold chains along with his accomplice in Ravet, Hinjewadi, Talegaon Dabhade and Vishrantwadi areas,” Fatangare said.The officer said the police have recovered stolen gold chains worth Rs1.65 lakh from him. Manlor was using a stolen motorcycle to commit the chain snatchings. He had stolen the motorcycle from Bidar in Karnataka. “Our investigations are on,” the officer said.
Article Summary: Aditya Manlor (19) has six cases of chain and mobile handset snatching registered against him. He admitted to have snatched gold chains along with his accomplice in Ravet, Hinjewadi, Talegaon Dabhade and Vishrantwadi areas.

Article Title: Arrested former corporator’s son booked in fresh attempted murder case
Article Text:
Nashik:





Deepak Londhe, son of former RPI corporator Prakash Londhe, has been booked in a fresh case of attempted murder inside the Nashik Road Central Prison.The case was registered following a complaint filed by a co-inmate who was recently released on bail. Deepak and his father have been lodged in judicial custody since Oct last year in connection with multiple cases.Deepak is among five individuals, including Harshad Patankar and lawyer Yateen Desle, who have been booked by the Nashik Road police under various sections of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS). The charges include sections 109 (attempted murder), 61 (2) (criminal conspiracy), 318(4) (cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property), 336(3) (forgery for the purpose of cheating) and 340(2) (possessing a forged document or electronic record).According to the police, the complainant, Mahesh Sonawane, alias Chimnya, was remanded to the Nashik Road prison on Jan 31 under the Maharashtra Control of Organised Crime Act (MCOCA). This followed an attempted murder case registered against him and others at the Ambad police station on Jan 30.Sonawane alleged that while he was in jail, Patankar repeatedly threatened him. Patankar accused Sonawane of giving statements to senior police officers that led to him being framed under MCOCA.Earlier this month, Patankar and Deepak approached Sonawane, stating they had drafted an application through their lawyer Desle. The application accused senior Nashik city police officers of falsely implicating them in various cases. They wanted Sonawane to sign the document.When Sonawane refused, Deepak, Patankar and others brutally assaulted him. They even tried to strangulate him. Fearing for his life, Sonawane ultimately signed the application.The matter came to light after Sonawane was released on bail. When police questioned him regarding the signed application against the department, he narrated the entire prison ordeal. Following his statement, a case was officially registered at the Ambad police station on Wednesday. The Nashik Road police are currently investigating the matter.
Article Summary: Deepak Londhe has been booked in a fresh case of attempted murder inside the Nashik Road Central Prison. The case was registered following a complaint filed by a co-inmate who was recently released on bail. Deepak and his father have been lodged in judicial custody since Oct last year in connection with multiple cases.

Article Title: Sengottaiyan flays previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies on Mekedatu dam issue
Article Text:
K A Sengottaiyan





ERODE: Tamil Nadu revenue minister K A Sengottaiyan on Saturday criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu.Speaking at a consultation meeting of officials from different departments held at the Erode district collectorate, Sengottaiyan reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move to build a dam in Mekedatu.During the review meeting, the minister told officials to deliver results quickly, saying the govt’s performance since coming to power warranted a thorough review and that the people’s demands should be met without delay. “Officers must complete necessary office procedures promptly and ensure citizens receive timely relief,” he told reporters after the meeting.The review, chaired by district collector S Kandasamy and also attended by handloom minister M Vijay Balaji, covered a wide range of administrative concerns.A major focus was the Athikadavu-Avinashi integrated drinking water project. Officials were directed to find out why more than 70 ponds were not getting filled and asked them to take remedial measures. Officials were instructed to begin silt-removal operations in the Bhavanisagar Dam to improve storage capacity and downstream supply.On allegations that liquor outlets were operating in violation of regulations, the revenue minister demanded concrete statistics and warned that strict action would follow where breaches were confirmed.
Article Summary: K A Sengottaiyan criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu. He reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move.

Article Title: Mere route change won’t attract illegal mining charges: HC
Article Text: Madurai: Mere deviation from the route mentioned in a valid transit pass while transporting minerals will not attract theft or illegal mining charges if there is no allegation of having committed them, Madurai bench of Madras high court has held.Justice R Vijayakumar made the observation while quashing an FIR registered by Palamedu police in Madurai district against Asaithambi, who was booked under Section 303(2) of BNS, 2023, and Section 21(5) of Mines and Minerals (Development and Regulation) Act, 1957.The case arose after the petitioner’s tractor carrying fencing stones was intercepted at Palamedu. The transit pass had permitted transport of fencing stones from Melur to Alanganallur, but the vehicle was found at Manikampatti, which police said was not on the permitted route.The court noted that the FIR itself showed the peitioner had a valid transit pass and that the only allegation was route deviation. “Hence, it is clear that there is only violation of the transit pass and there is no theft of fencing stones or any minerals belonging to the govt,” the judge said.Holding that invoking the penal provisions was not legally sustainable, the court said continuation of proceedings would be an abuse of process of law, and quashed the FIR.
Article Summary: Mere deviation from the route mentioned in a valid transit pass while transporting minerals will not attract theft or illegal mining charges, Madurai bench of Madras high court has held. Justice R Vijayakumar made the observation while quashing an FIR registered by Palamedu police in Madurai district.

Article Title: Keralite man found dead in Tamil Nadu; suicide suspected
Article Text:
Palakkad

:





In a suspected case of suicide, a 47-year-old tanker truck driver was found dead at Vadakkipalayam in Tamil Nadu , close to Kerala border, on Thursday morning.The deceased was identified as Radhakrishna Pillai, a native of Kollam. Locals found him hanging from the rear ladder of a tanker truck parked there and immediately alerted police.According to preliminary investigations, the tanker truck was parked there since Wednesday night. Locals noticed Pillai hanging on Thursday morning, following which Vadakkipalayam police reached the spot and conducted an inspection.Police contacted the owner of the tanker truck, which is registered in Kottayam, and collected details about the deceased. The exact reason behind the incident is yet to be ascertained.The body was shifted to govt hospital mortuary in Palani for postmortem. Police have registered a case and launched an investigation into the circumstances surrounding the death.Suicides are preventable and help is just a phone call away. Those in distress can call Maitri at 0484-2540530 or emergency medical services at 0471- 2552056.
Article Summary: Radhakrishna Pillai, a 47-year-old tanker truck driver was found dead at Vadakkipalayam in Tamil Nadu, close to Kerala border. Locals found him hanging from the rear ladder of a tanker truck parked there and immediately alerted police. The exact reason behind the incident is yet to be ascertained.

Article Title: As Hungary's Magyar joins EU summit, sidelined Orban meets with far-right allies in Brussels
Article Text: Fidesz party head and former prime minister Viktor Orban, center, speaks to the press as he arrives at the opposition Fidesz party's working congress meeting, in Budapest. (AP Photo)
Article Summary: Viktor Orban is the head of the opposition Fidesz party in Hungary. Orban was prime minister of Hungary from 1998 to 2008. He is now the leader of the anti- government Jobbik party, which is also in opposition.
